text:
There was once a little old lady who dearly loved cattails. There was one particular kind she liked a lot -- it had bright orange eyes. She would sit by the river all summer long collecting those shiny orange seeds.

Once, she got out her basket and saw something squish under the edge. Her heart sank! It was another little old lady's cat – this one brown with floppy ears too big for his face - curled up quietly at the edge. She gently picked him up.

"This is Leo," she said proudly. "He won't hurt you." Her voice was soft and soothing. She took him to the park where she hoped he'd make some friends.

Leo scampered along the grassy paths. Kids ran behind him bouncing balls. A little girl came up laughing. She looked at Leo.

"You're purrrfect," she cooed. Leo bopped the girl on the shoulder. She giggled delightedly then scuffled away towards a bench.
